reasoning: The interviewer's question about message types and conversation modes directly elicited two hidden implicit requirements: IR3 (sending text, images, videos, and files) was revealed when the user specified the content types, and IR2 (adding friends and creating groups) was revealed when the user confirmed support for both one-on-one and group conversations.

The interviewer asked about message delivery status indicators and push notifications, which are not related to any of the remaining hidden requirements (IR1: registration/login, IR4: voice/video calls, IR5: light blue background with dark blue elements). The user's response also did not reveal any of the hidden requirements, as they simply deflected the question and asked for more specific questions about core functionality, user interactions, or visual design.

The interviewer asked about the visual style or theme of the messaging app, and the user responded that they prefer the background color to be light blue and all elements to be dark blue. This directly matches IR5, which specifies the same color scheme preference. IR1 (registration/login) and IR4 (voice/video calls) were not addressed by this exchange.
